tldr: Headlines collectively assert a contained but growing global health emergency — three dead, eleven confirmed cases — with quarantine mandates rolling out across Europe and Asia-Pacific; a parallel sub-plot about misinformation, vaccine-funding gaps, and political blame is also visible from titles alone.

evidence.quote: More cases of hantavirus coming – WHO chief
Three dead, but no new infections
No sign of larger outbreak of hantavirus says WHO chief
ECDC urges quarantine for passengers on KLM flight
Indonesia alerts quarantine offices over hantavirus threat
France tightens health surveillance on deadly Hantavirus cases
Scientists ran out of funds while developing a vaccine
Health body investigating hantavirus hit by fresh Milei budget cuts
Korean frontier in discovery, prevention of hantavirus returns to spotlight
Amid Hantavirus Panic, the Ivermectin Super Fans Are Back
Complotti e allarmismo, il circo dell'Hantavirus
face à l'hantavirus, entre anxiété et critique des médias

isolation_top.note: Single bare headline ('Hantavirus cruise outbreak grows to 11') — pure count update with no context, semantically thin
Headline foregrounds Korean historical role in hantavirus discovery — retrospective scientific-legacy angle absent from other outlets
New Yorker leads with a first-person scientist narrative; second headline (high-school debate) is entirely off-topic and inflates distance
SMH headline buries hantavirus in an omnibus 'Australia LIVE' roundup alongside unrelated domestic politics — content artefact, not editorial isolation
Headline advertises a precautionary alert with no documented cases — epidemiological-prevention framing distinct from outbreak coverage

silences.what_they_covered_instead: The French health minister's announcements (all contacts tested negative) appear only via wire_services and are cited by multiple other outlets, but no dedicated france bucket is in the corpus
Brazil is the largest South American country adjacent to the Andes hantavirus geographic range but filed nothing in this corpus
Large Arabic-language health-alert readership absent despite the story reaching global scale; Arabic coverage not captured in monitoring

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only outlet to name 'Patient Zero' by role and location in its headline — personalises outbreak origin in a way absent from all other titles
Only outlet whose headline explicitly names the ivermectin misinformation wave — flags a disinformation sub-story invisible in all other buckets
Only outlet advertising North Korea's internal public-health warning to citizens — the sole window into DPRK domestic framing of the outbreak
Only outlet linking the outbreak response directly to domestic austerity (Milei budget cuts) in its headline — political economy angle absent elsewhere

bottom_line: Headline coverage frames the MV Hondius outbreak as a manageable public-health alert with containment underway, but misinformation resurgence and vaccine-funding failures signal anxieties that the health-safety frame alone cannot hold.
